reasoning: The baseline price is set on Friday, November 7 (for Nov 9). Paramount Skydance (PSKY) is scheduled to report Q3 earnings on November 10, the day after the baseline. The stock has been trading near $15.88, which is considerably higher than the consensus analyst price targets of around $10.50 - $11.58 set in August/September (though one outlier target is $17). The stock recently experienced 'meme-stock' volatility and a large post-merger run-up. While the impending details of a $2 billion cost-cutting plan (and 2,000-3,000 job cuts) could support the stock on the Nov 10 earnings call, this news has been public since August and is likely priced in, raising the risk of a "sell-the-news" pullback. Additionally, retail-driven hype stocks often face downward mean reversion over time. Therefore, the short-term probability of being above the baseline price is slightly below 50%. As per the instructions, uncertainty increases over longer forecast horizons, bringing the probabilities for distant dates closer to 0.50.
